title: Unexpected fillings, singularities, and plane curve arrangements
speaker: Laura Starkston
abstract: I will discuss joint work with Olga Plamenevskaya studying symplectic fillings of links of certain complex surface singularities, and comparing symplectic fillings with complex smoothings. We develop characterizations of the symplectic fillings using planar Lefschetz fibrations and singular braided surfaces. This provides an analogue of de Jong and van Straten's work which characterizes the complex smoothings in terms of decorated complex plane curves.

proof_stages.label: Surface-singularity links and natural fillings
proof_stages.summary: The talk sets up an isolated complex surface singularity, its contact three-dimensional link, and the algebraic-geometric fillings supplied by Milnor-fiber smoothings and by resolutions, leading to the question of whether all symplectic fillings arise this way.

proof_stages.label: Motivation from known filling classifications
proof_stages.summary: Known classifications for simple, simple elliptic, cyclic, quotient, and related singularities mostly identify symplectic fillings with resolutions or smoothings, while higher-complexity nonrational examples show that genuinely non-algebraic fillings can occur.

proof_stages.label: Reduced-fundamental-cycle rational singularities
proof_stages.summary: The target class is defined combinatorially by genus-zero negative-definite plumbing trees satisfying the valence/self-intersection inequality, and its boundary contact manifolds are exactly the planar-open-book cases amenable to Wendl’s filling framework.

proof_stages.label: Existence and absence theorems for unexpected RFC fillings
proof_stages.summary: The main theorem asserts RFC singularities with arbitrarily many nondiffeomorphic rel-boundary unexpected symplectic fillings, even simply connected ones, while a complementary result says sufficiently negative Euler labels force only the resolution-type filling.

proof_stages.label: de Jong–van Straten reduction and symplectic analogue
proof_stages.summary: The proof strategy reduces smoothings of the surface singularity to complex deformations of an associated plane-curve singularity and replaces these, on the symplectic side, by graphical homotopies of the plane-curve components.

proof_stages.label: Weighted plane-curve data from resolution graphs
proof_stages.summary: An augmented plumbing graph is blown down to a weighted plane-curve germ, and allowable endpoints of either complex deformations or graphical homotopies are transverse curve arrangements satisfying the marking and weight constraints.

proof_stages.label: Curve arrangements produce Lefschetz-fibration fillings
proof_stages.summary: After blowing up the marked points of a deformed curve arrangement and deleting the strict transform, the projection to one coordinate becomes a planar Lefschetz fibration, yielding Milnor fibers in the complex case and symplectic fillings for graphical homotopies.

proof_stages.label: Projective-incidence obstructions to complex deformations
proof_stages.summary: Using Pappus-type incidence constraints, the proof constructs graphical arrangements that cannot arise from complex analytic deformations, and the resulting incidence data distinguish the corresponding fillings from all Milnor fibers.
